'''Craig Murdock''' (born 24 October 1973) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|SH}}. Murdock played for [[Hensingham A.R.L.F.C.|Hensingham]] [[Hensingham ARLFC|ARLFC]] (in [[Hensingham]], [[Whitehaven]]),<ref name="Hensingham ARLFC at pitchero.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.pitchero.com/clubs/hensinghamarlfc|title=Hensingham ARLFC at pitchero.com|publisher=pitchero.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Hull F.C.|Hull Sharks]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

==Playing career==
Born in [[Cumbria]], Murdock started his professional career with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 1993 after being signed from amateur side Hensingham. He scored 23 tries in 68 appearances for the club, and took part in the [[Clash of the Codes (rugby)|Clash of the Codes]] match against [[Bath Rugby|Bath]], scoring two tries in the match played under [[rugby union]] rules.<ref>{{cite web|last=Bale|first=Steve|title=Related attractions|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/related-attractions-1349395.html|work=The Independent|accessdate=29 November 2013|date=27 May 1996}}</ref>

In 1998, he joined [[Hull F.C.|Hull Sharks]], initially on loan before signing a permanent deal.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Rugby League: Loan star Murdock shines|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-loan-star-murdock-shines-1166778.html|website=The Independent|accessdate=17 July 2017|date=21 June 1998}}</ref> He also went on to play for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]

==Media career==
After his playing career ended, Murdock worked as a commentator for [[BBC Radio Humberside]] for almost a decade, but was forced to leave the role in 2014 due to a "conflict of interest" with his work as a player agent.<ref>{{cite web|title=Former Hull FC and Hull KR star Craig Murdock leaves BBC commentary role over 'conflict of interest'|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-Hull-KR-star-Craig-Murdock-leaves-BBC/story-21457585-detail/story.html|website=Hull Daily Mail|publisher=Local World|accessdate=17 July 2017|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140717055026/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-Hull-KR-star-Craig-Murdock-leaves-BBC/story-21457585-detail/story.html|archivedate=17 July 2014|date=14 July 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> He was subsequently employed by the ''[[Hull Daily Mail]]'' as a columnist.<ref>{{cite web|title=Former Hull FC and Hull KR player Craig Murdock joins Hull Daily Mail as columnist|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk:80/Hull-FC-Hull-KR-player-Craig-Murdock-joins-Hull/story-21656171-detail/story.html|website=Hull Daily Mail|publisher=Local World|accessdate=17 July 2017|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140720031409/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-Hull-KR-player-Craig-Murdock-joins-Hull/story-21656171-detail/story.html|archivedate=20 July 2014|date=18 July 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Honours==
* [[RFL Championship]]: 1994–95, 1995–96
* [[RFL Premiership|Premiership]]: 1996
